label: Hwasong-17 ICBM (Nov 18, 2022)
notes: Launched from Sunan/Pyongyang. Lofted trajectory: ~6,000 km apogee, ~1,000 km range. Splashed in Sea of Japan within Japan's EEZ ~200 km west of Oshima Island. Maximum range estimated 15,000+ km.

label: Hwasong-15 ICBM (Nov 28, 2017)
notes: Launched from Pyongsong area. Reached ~4,475 km altitude on lofted trajectory; flew ~960 km horizontally. Splashed ~250 km west of Hokkaido. Kim declared nuclear deterrence complete.

label: Hwasong-14 ICBM — 1st Test (Jul 4, 2017)
notes: Launched from Panghyon/Kusong. Reached ~2,802 km altitude; flew ~933 km. First confirmed ICBM test. Kim dubbed it a 'gift to American bastards' on US Independence Day.

label: Hwasong-12 IRBM over Japan (Aug 29, 2017)
notes: Launched from Sunan. Flew over Hokkaido, Japan triggering J-Alert emergency warnings to 6 million people. Range ~2,700 km. Japan called it 'unprecedented threat'. Estimated range 4,500–5,000 km threatening Guam.

label: Musudan (Hwasong-10) IRBM Test (Jun 22, 2016)
notes: One successful test after four failures in 2016. Reached 1,413 km altitude on lofted trajectory; flew ~400 km. Demonstrated partial IRBM capability. Range of 3,000–4,000 km could reach Guam and US bases in Japan.

label: KN-23 SRBM Tests (Multiple, 2019–2022)
notes: DPRK test-fired KN-23 (Hwasong-11) short-range ballistic missiles dozens of times 2019–2022. Solid-fueled, Iskander-type, low-altitude depressed trajectory to evade missile defense. Range 450–690 km. Examples recovered in Ukraine 2024 after DPRK supply to Russia confirmed.

label: NKPA Invasion — 38th Parallel to Seoul (Jun 25, 1950)
notes: Korean People's Army crossed the 38th parallel at dawn June 25, 1950, beginning the Korean War. 75,000 NKPA troops with Soviet T-34 tanks overwhelmed 38,000 ill-equipped ROK forces. Seoul fell on June 28. UN Security Council (with Soviet Union absent) authorized military intervention.
